name: Danish Blue Cheese Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Use 1 loaf (11 oz) of thinly sliced pumpernickel bread.
2. Preheat the oven to 250°F. Dry the bread slices in the oven until they crumble easily (about 20-25 minutes).
3. Melt butter and mix with sugar. Crush the bread, adding butter and sugar, then mix well.
4. Press crumbs into a pie tin. Raise temperature to 35°F and bake crust for 15 minutes. Cool before filling.
5. In a saucepan, combine gelatin and water, stirring until clear (about 6-8 minutes). Let cool.
6. Beat cream cheese until light and smooth. Mash blue cheese and mix with cream cheese.
7. Add gelatin mixture to the cheese and fold in. Whip cream until stiff and fold into the mixture.
8. Pour filling gently into prepared crust, pressing grapes upright (tops showing).
9. Chill pie for several hours or until set.
